*Information about this image and its acquisition is forthcoming.*

NGC 281, nicknamed the Pacman Nebula for the dark dust lane that gives it a wedge-shaped "mouth," is an emission nebula and young open cluster in the constellation Cassiopeia. It lies about 9,200 light-years away, shines around magnitude 7.4, and spans roughly 35 by 30 arcminutes.

*This description was generated by an AI assistant from general astronomical references, not from Jay's own notes on this image — treat it as a starting point, not a verified caption.*
